title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has officially responded to a 2020 report recommending public standards for Artificial Intelligence, outlining their approach to addressing these concerns. This response aims to implement and maintain safety and ethics within AI systems.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This policy document is a report and assessment on the use of copyrighted materials in the creation of artificial intelligence systems, exploring their impact. It considers how copyright laws affect the development and use of AI.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: This policy outlines the Department for Education's stance on the use of generative AI in educational settings. It aims to establish a framework for safe and effective use of generative AI tools in teaching and learning.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This document outlines a plan for the Department for Transport (DfT) to use Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the transport sector, taking advantage of its benefits while addressing potential risks. The plan aims to work with the transport industry to harness AI's potential.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: The copyright policy discusses the use of copyrighted materials in the development of artificial intelligence systems and provides an update on progress related to this issue.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is responding to a council's recommendations on how AI should be regulated when used in medical devices. The response will address how AI should be approved, monitored, and used in healthcare settings.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: Ofqual is establishing a regulatory approach to ensure artificial intelligence is used fairly and safely in exams and qualifications. This includes responding to the request from government ministers for guidance on the issue.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to a report from the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence, calling for continued action to ensure benefits are realized from artificial intelligence without compromising public safety and values. The response aims to address the report's recommendations for regulating AI and mitigating its risks in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This policy document outlines the scope and objectives of a committee's review of artificial intelligence and its impact on public standards, providing a framework for their investigation. The document is intended to serve as a guiding reference for the committee's work, answering key questions and establishing the terms of their inquiry.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The UK's Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has provided written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry into governing Artificial Intelligence. This submission aims to address the governance of AI, likely discussing its implications and potential regulations in the UK.
